During preparation of 2016 report, error identified in calculating volume of flared natural gas in previous years. This impacted the amount of VOC released.

Also, changes to estimation methods for calculating fugitive emissions impacted the amount of VOC released.

comments.comment: Amount discharged in dependent on the concentration of arsenic in produced water and the volume of produced water discharged. The concentration detected in the produced water in 2018 resulted in the amount of arsenic discharged in produced water above the reporting threshold. Note there was also a small amount of arsenic (0.179 kg) associated with lead acid battery recycling.

comments.comment: Majority of VOCs in previous years has been attributed to fugitive emissions which were calculated using a generic component methodology (CAPP 2005/2007). Fugitives in 2023 determined from leaks detected via Leak Detection and Repair surveys, resulting in a 91% reduction in reported VOCs.

reasonsForPlan.en: Plan was prepared or implemented for another government jurisdiction or Parliament (i.e. provincial government)
reasonsForPlan.fr: Plan préparé ou mis en œuvre pour une autre autorité gouvernementale (c.-à-d., autre ministère fédéral, province, municipalité)

p2Plan.planComment: Environmental Protection and Compliance Monitoring Plan as required by the Canada-Newfoundland and Labrador Offshore Petroleum Board (C-NLOPB) as a condition for an Operating Authorization.

reasonsForNoActivities.fr: Autres (précisez dans le champ des commentaires)

p2Plan.noActivitiesComment: Although a formal P2 plan is not in place, Cenovus's Environmental Protection and Compliance Monitoring Plan and Continuous Improvement standard promotes reducing waste streams to as low as reasonably practicable (ALARP). This is evident in the continued reduction in GHG emissions and low oil-in-water concentrations in produced water.
